tutorial.step1.title: Welcome to Transcript Library
tutorial.step1.desc: This is where all your transcripts are stored. You can search previous sermons, review scripture matches, download transcripts, and start new recording sessions.
tutorial.step2.title: Search Transcripts
tutorial.step2.desc: Use the search box to quickly find transcripts. You can search by sermon title, keywords, scripture references, or any text spoken during a session.
tutorial.step3.title: Overview Cards
tutorial.step3.desc: These cards give you a quick overview of your transcript activity — total sessions, usage this month, scriptures detected, and total duration.
tutorial.step4.title: Filters
tutorial.step4.desc: Filters help you find transcripts faster. Filter by language, date range, or service type.
tutorial.step5.title: Transcript List
tutorial.step5.desc: Every transcript appears here. Each row shows the session name, recording date, duration, scripture matches, and language.
tutorial.step6.title: Download Transcript
tutorial.step6.desc: You can download any transcript for offline use. Downloads are available in supported export formats.
tutorial.step7.title: More Actions
tutorial.step7.desc: Additional actions are available here. Depending on the transcript you may be able to rename, delete, export, or view details.
tutorial.step8.title: Switch Views
tutorial.step8.desc: Switch between List View and Grid View. List View shows detailed information. Grid View is useful when browsing many transcripts visually.
tutorial.step9.title: Start a New Session
tutorial.step9.desc: Ready to create a new transcript? Click New Session to start Speech to Scripture and begin recording.
tutorial.final.title: You're Ready!
tutorial.final.desc: You now know how to use the Transcript Library. Your transcripts are automatically saved and available here anytime.
tutorial.button.tooltip: Start the interactive tutorial for this page
detailTutorial.button.tooltip: Start the interactive tutorial for this page
detailTutorial.step1.title: Transcript Detail
detailTutorial.step1.desc: This page shows the full transcript with timestamps, detected scripture references, and translation options. Let's walk through the key features.
detailTutorial.step2.title: Back Navigation
detailTutorial.step2.desc: Use this button to return to the Transcript Library. You can always come back to this transcript later.
detailTutorial.step3.title: Transcript Metadata
detailTutorial.step3.desc: This row shows the transcript title, church name, recording date, duration, and language. This information is automatically captured during the session.
detailTutorial.step3.action: Review the metadata, then click Next
detailTutorial.step4.title: Export Transcript
detailTutorial.step4.desc: Export your transcript as a PDF or Word document. Choose the format that works best for sharing or archiving.
detailTutorial.step5.title: Translate Transcript
detailTutorial.step5.desc: Translate this transcript into another language. You can translate the full transcript or just the detected scripture references.
detailTutorial.step6.title: Search Within Transcript
detailTutorial.step6.desc: Use the search box to find specific words, phrases, or scripture references within this transcript.
detailTutorial.step7.title: Copy Transcript
detailTutorial.step7.desc: Copy the entire transcript text to your clipboard for pasting into another app or document.
detailTutorial.step8.title: Transcript Content
detailTutorial.step8.desc: The full transcript is displayed here with timestamps. Scripture references are highlighted in color. Click any line to see more context.
detailTutorial.step9.title: Sidebar Panel
detailTutorial.step9.desc: The right sidebar shows detected scriptures and saved translations. Switch between tabs to explore different information.
detailTutorial.step10.title: Translations
detailTutorial.step10.desc: View and manage your saved translations. You can export, copy, or view each translation from here.
detailTutorial.final.title: You're Ready!
detailTutorial.final.desc: You now know how to navigate the Transcript Detail page. Your transcripts are always available for review and export.

themeSettings.tour.step1.title: Welcome to Theme Settings
themeSettings.tour.step1.desc: This is your control center for all visual themes — the look and feel of your Bible display, lower thirds, and scrolling tickers during services.
themeSettings.tour.step1.action: Click Next to continue the tour
themeSettings.tour.step2.title: Custom Themes
themeSettings.tour.step2.desc: The Custom tab is where you create and manage your own themes. You can design unique styles that match your church's branding — colors, fonts, backgrounds, and more.
themeSettings.tour.step2.action: Click Next to see how to create a theme
themeSettings.tour.step3.title: Create a Theme
themeSettings.tour.step3.desc: Click this button to open the Theme Creator. You'll pick a template, choose your colors and fonts, add a background image or video, and save it — all without writing any code.
themeSettings.tour.step3.action: Click Next to see where saved themes appear
themeSettings.tour.step4.title: Your Saved Themes
themeSettings.tour.step4.desc: Every theme you create shows up here. You can edit, duplicate, or delete them. Your custom themes are automatically available in OBS when you set up your production scenes.
themeSettings.tour.step4.action: Click Next to explore OBS themes
themeSettings.tour.step5.title: Built-In OBS Themes
themeSettings.tour.step5.desc: The OBS tab has a library of professionally designed lower-third themes. These are ready to use — just preview them and add your favorites to OBS with one click.
themeSettings.tour.step5.action: Click Next to learn about previewing themes
themeSettings.tour.step6.title: Preview Themes
themeSettings.tour.step6.desc: Click the expand button on any theme card to see a full-size preview. This lets you check how the text, colors, and animations will look on screen before sending it to OBS.
themeSettings.tour.step6.action: Click Next to see how to send themes to OBS
themeSettings.tour.step7.title: Send to OBS
themeSettings.tour.step7.desc: Click the star button to add a theme to your OBS favorites. Favorited themes are automatically synced to your Dock and appear in the lower-third picker during services.
themeSettings.tour.step7.action: Click Next to explore scrolling tickers
themeSettings.tour.step8.title: Scrolling Tickers
themeSettings.tour.step8.desc: The Tickers tab has scrolling text banners for church announcements, prayer requests, contact info, and more. These run along the bottom of your OBS output during services.
themeSettings.tour.step8.action: Click Next to learn about ticker previews
themeSettings.tour.step9.title: Preview Tickers
themeSettings.tour.step9.desc: Click the expand button to see how a ticker will scroll on screen. You can check the speed, colors, and layout before adding it to your production setup.
themeSettings.tour.step9.action: Click Next to see how to install tickers
themeSettings.tour.step10.title: Install a Ticker
themeSettings.tour.step10.desc: Click the star button to add a ticker to your favorites. It will automatically sync to your Dock and be ready to use during your next service.
themeSettings.tour.step10.action: Click Next to finish the tour
themeSettings.tour.final.title: You're All Set!
themeSettings.tour.final.desc: Here's what you can do in Theme Settings:
themeSettings.tour.final.check1: Create custom themes with your church's branding
themeSettings.tour.final.check2: Browse and preview professional OBS themes
themeSettings.tour.final.check3: Add favorites to sync themes to OBS instantly
themeSettings.tour.final.check4: Set up scrolling tickers for announcements
themeSettings.tour.final.check5: Edit or delete any theme at any time
themeSettings.tour.button.tooltip: Start the interactive tutorial for this page
mgt.step1.title: Welcome to Multi-View Layouts
mgt.step1.desc: Browse and manage multi-view layouts for OBS. Display multiple cameras, speakers, scriptures, and other content simultaneously.
mgt.step2.title: Search Layouts
mgt.step2.desc: Use the search box to quickly find layouts by name, description, or use case.
mgt.step3.title: Category Filters
mgt.step3.desc: Filter layouts by category to find the right one for your service. You can also view layouts you've already added.
mgt.step4.title: Layout Grid
mgt.step4.desc: Browse available layouts in the grid. Each card shows a preview, the number of slots, and use cases.
mgt.step5.title: Add to OBS
mgt.step5.desc: Preview a layout to see its details, then add it to OBS with one click. Added layouts are marked in green.
mgt.step5.action: Click Preview or Add to OBS
mgt.final.title: You're Ready!
mgt.final.desc: You now know how to browse and install multi-view layouts. These layouts help you display multiple content sources in OBS.
spt.step1.title: Welcome to Service Planner
spt.step1.desc: Build the service run-down before you go live. Plan Bible verses, worship cues, sermon points, and media as one ordered list.
spt.step2.title: Create a Plan
spt.step2.desc: Click New plan to create a new service plan. Each plan holds an ordered list of cues for the dock operator.
spt.step3.title: Plans Sidebar
spt.step3.desc: Switch between different service plans. Each plan shows its date and number of cues.
spt.step3.action: Click any plan in the sidebar
spt.step4.title: Plan Editor
spt.step4.desc: Set the plan title, service date, and status. You can also duplicate or delete plans.
spt.step4.action: Review the editor fields, then click Next
spt.step5.title: Run-Down Cues
spt.step5.desc: Your ordered list of cues appears here. Reorder, edit, or remove cues. Click a cue in the dock to preview it.
spt.step6.title: Add Cues
spt.step6.desc: Add Bible references, worship songs, sermon quotes or points, and media items as cues. Each cue becomes available in the dock.
spt.step6.action: Select a cue type and fill in the details
spt.final.title: You're Ready!
spt.final.desc: You now know how to build a service run-down. Your plans are synced with the MakeChurchEasy Dock for live control.
stt.step1.title: Welcome to Speech to Scripture
stt.step1.desc: Real-time speech-to-verse detection. Speak or play audio and watch as Bible references are detected and matched automatically.
stt.step2.title: Choose Microphone
stt.step2.desc: Select the microphone input for transcription. Click the dropdown to see available devices.
stt.step3.title: Start Listening
stt.step3.desc: Click Start Listening to begin real-time transcription. The system will detect Bible references as speech is captured.
stt.step4.title: Live Transcript
stt.step4.desc: The live transcript appears here in real time. Each segment shows a timestamp and the detected text.
stt.step4.action: Watch the transcript as it updates
stt.step5.title: Top Match
stt.step5.desc: The best matching Bible verse is displayed here. Push it to OBS with one click to show it live.
stt.step5.action: Review the top match, then click Next
stt.step6.title: Candidate Matches
stt.step6.desc: Other possible verse matches appear here. Select any candidate to push it to OBS instead of the top match.
stt.final.title: You're Ready!
stt.final.desc: You now know how to use Speech to Scripture. Start listening and let AI detect Bible references in real time.
rt.step1.title: Welcome to Resources
rt.step1.desc: Manage Bible translations, worship songs, and media assets from one place. These resources power the MakeChurchEasy Dock.
rt.step2.title: Resource Tabs
rt.step2.desc: Switch between Bible, Worship, and Media tabs. Each tab contains tools for managing that type of resource.
rt.step3.title: Tab Content
rt.step3.desc: Each tab shows the management interface for that resource type. Download translations, manage songs, or organize media.
rt.step3.action: Explore the content, then click Next
rt.final.title: You're Ready!
rt.final.desc: You now know how to manage resources. Bible translations, worship songs, and media are all synced with the dock.
rt.button.tooltip: Start the interactive tutorial for this page
mgt.button.tooltip: Start the interactive tutorial for this page
spt.button.tooltip: Start the interactive tutorial for this page
stt.button.tooltip: Start the interactive tutorial for this page
dt.step1.title: Welcome to Your Dashboard
dt.step1.desc: This is your home base in MakeChurchEasy. Here you can see at a glance whether OBS is connected, access all your modules, and track recent activity.
dt.step1.action: Click Next to continue the tour
dt.step2.title: OBS Connection Status
dt.step2.desc: This panel shows whether OBS Studio and the MakeChurchEasy Dock are connected. Click "Connect to OBS" to get started — you'll need OBS running for live features to work.
dt.step2.action: Click Next to see the module cards
dt.step3.title: Quick Access Modules
dt.step3.desc: These four cards give you instant access to Voice Bible, Bible, Worship songs, and Media. Each card shows a quick status — like how many translations or songs you have loaded.
dt.step3.action: Click Next to learn about Voice Bible
dt.step4.title: Voice Bible
dt.step4.desc: Voice Bible lets you speak a scripture reference out loud and it displays instantly on screen. Click "Start Listening" to activate it — the mic icon will pulse while it's listening.
dt.step4.action: Click Next to see connection URLs
dt.step5.title: Connection URLs
dt.step5.desc: These URLs are what you add to OBS as Custom Browser Docks. Copy the Bible Overlay URL and the Scripture Assistant URL into OBS to set up your live production workflow.
dt.step5.action: Click Next to see the activity log
dt.step6.title: Activity & System Status
dt.step6.desc: The activity log tracks what you've done recently — connections, song additions, and more. The system status panel shows the health of every connected service at a glance.
dt.step6.action: Click Next to finish the tour
dt.final.title: You're All Set!
dt.final.desc: Here's what you can do from the dashboard:
dt.final.check1: Connect to OBS for live production
dt.final.check2: Access Bible, Worship, and Media modules instantly
dt.final.check3: Use Voice Bible for hands-free scripture display
dt.final.check4: Set up OBS browser docks with connection URLs
dt.final.check5: Track your activity and system health
